item.title: Market Regime: 🟡 Mixed Signals
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Mixed signals dominate the tape. Equities are just under long-term resistance (SPY below its 200‑day MA) while tech (QQQ) remains below its 100‑day MA — a cautious setup for equity bulls. Interest rates are materially higher (TNX 4.31%), which increases discount‑rate pressure on growth stocks and raises refinancing costs for leveraged borrowers. Volatility structure is conflicted: VIX sits in a mid‑to‑elevated zone (~23.9) — not panic but higher than typical calm markets — while OVX is very high (93.1), signaling strong oil/energy risk or supply concerns that could feed inflationary pressures. Taken together, these factors argue for risk discipline: favor size and exposure control, tighter stops, and selective participation rather than broad risk-on positioning.
Market summary (bullets): - Regime: 🟡 Mixed Signals — conditions show no clear directional consensus. - SPY: 655.83 vs 200‑day MA 662.58 — trading below the 200MA (bearish bias). - QQQ: 584.98 vs 100‑day MA 609.63 — below key shorter-term trend (tech weakness). - ARKK: 3‑month change −12.45% — recent drawdown but not flagged as triggering the ARKK_3mo_drop signal. - VIX: 23.87 — elevated but below extreme panic (>25); VIX_over_25 false, VIX_under_20 false. - TNX: 4.31% — above 4% (TNX_above_4 true), signaling tighter financial conditions. - OVX: 93.14 — regime high (OVX_high true), indicating large oil volatility risk. - Signals stable: the provided signal set has not changed — conditions are stable relative to the last check. - Trading implication: prioritize risk management, reduce size in discretionary long bets, consider hedges for rate and energy volatility exposure, and look for clearer trend confirmation before adding directional exposure.
Data files: - Raw data files referenced under /data (use these for source verification and deeper analysis).

item.title: Market Regime: 🟡 Mixed Signals
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Market conditions show mixed signals with elevated commodity volatility and higher yields adding risk to an otherwise range-bound equity backdrop. OVX is very high (91.8), flagging oil/energy-driven volatility risk that can spill into equity sentiment and inflation expectations. TNX at 4.33% keeps fixed-income yields elevated, pressuring growth/technology names and raising funding costs. Equity breadth looks soft: SPY remains below its 200‑day MA and QQQ is under its 100‑day MA, indicating the medium-term trend for large caps and tech is not bullish. VIX near 24.5 is elevated but not extreme; combined with high OVX and high yields, this argues for defensive positioning and tighter risk controls. Conditions are stable (no change in the signal set).
Market summary (bullets): - Regime label: 🟡 Mixed Signals — equities showing weakness amid macro/commodity stress. - Equities: - SPY: 655.24, 200‑day MA 662.31 → trading below 200MA (bearish medium-term). - QQQ: 584.31, 100‑day MA 609.89 → trading below 100MA (tech momentum weak). - ARKK 3‑month change: -11.08% → not flagged as an extreme drop in the signals, but notable decline. - Volatility: - VIX: 24.54 → elevated, signaling higher equity option-implied volatility. - OVX: 91.8 (regime: high) → very high oil volatility; key risk for inflation/energy sectors. - Rates: - TNX (10‑yr yield): 4.33% → above 4%, a material upward pressure on discount rates and cyclical/growth valuations. - Risk posture: Favor defensive allocation, manage position sizes and stop levels, consider hedges for commodity-driven inflation shocks and rate-sensitive exposure. - Data/files: raw input and snapshot files located in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🔴 Downturn Risk
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Market risk is elevated — equity indices are below key moving averages, volatility is high, and yields remain elevated. VIX at 25.25 and OVX in the mid regime indicate persistent fear and commodity-related uncertainty. With SPY under its 200‑day MA and QQQ under its 100‑day MA, downside momentum is present. Conditions are stable relative to the last read (signals have not changed).
Market summary: - Equities: SPY 650.34 (200‑day MA 662.02) — trading below its 200‑day moving average. - Tech/large growth: QQQ 577.18 (100‑day MA 610.28) — trading below its 100‑day moving average. - Thematic: ARKK three‑month change −13.2% (weakness but not a fresh extreme signal). - Volatility: VIX 25.25 — above 25, elevated market fear. - Rates: TNX yield 4.30% — above 4%, signaling tighter financial conditions. - Oil volatility: OVX 89.39 — in the mid regime, indicating meaningful oil-market uncertainty. - Signals stable: No signal changes since the prior snapshot. - Data files: raw inputs referenced are available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🔴 Downturn Risk
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: The market is in a clear downturn-risk regime. Major equity benchmarks are trading well below their longer-term moving averages, volatility is elevated across equities and oil, and Treasury yields are sitting materially higher — a classic risk-off backdrop that favors protection and liquidity. With VIX above 25, OVX in the high regime, and TNX above 4%, downside sensitivity and cross-asset stress remain elevated. Consider reducing exposure to beta, tightening position sizing, and prioritizing convex downside protection until volatility and breadth improve.
Market summary: - Regime: 🔴 Downturn Risk — conditions remain stable (no signal changes). - Equities: - SPY 631.97 vs 200MA 661.79 — trading below the 200-day moving average. - QQQ 558.28 vs 100MA 610.71 — trading below the 100-day moving average. - ARKK three-month change: -19.2% — significant drawdown in high-growth thematic exposure. - Volatility and commodities: - VIX: 30.61 — elevated equity volatility (VIX_over_25 = true). - OVX: 95.84 (regime: high) — oil volatility very high, signaling stress in energy markets. - Rates: - TNX yield: 4.35% — higher rates (TNX_above_4 = true), tightening financial conditions. - Files / data: - Raw data referenced: /data (please check the files in /data for the underlying time series and inputs).
If you’d like, I can suggest specific hedges, cash levels, or a checklist for reducing portfolio risk.

item.title: Market Regime: 🔴 Downturn Risk
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Downturn risk is elevated. Equities are below key moving averages, volatility is well above normal, oil-volatility is extreme, and short-term interest rates are high — a classic risk-off cocktail that can accelerate downside and increase dispersion. Position sizing, tighter risk controls, and liquidity considerations are warranted. Conditions are stable versus the previous signal set (no change).
Market summary (snapshot): - SPY: 634.09 vs 200‑day MA 661.63 — trading below the 200MA, bearish technical backdrop. - QQQ: 562.58 vs 100‑day MA 611.44 — below the 100MA, tech leadership under pressure. - ARKK: -18.91% over 3 months — significant drawdown in high-beta/innovation exposure. - VIX: 31.05 — elevated equity-market implied volatility (>25), signaling heightened fear. - TNX (10‑yr yield): 4.44% — higher yields (>4%) increase discount rates and financing costs. - OVX: 96.64 (regime: high) — extreme oil-volatility regime, adding commodity and energy market stress.
Data files: Raw data used in this assessment are in /data.
